# LYNQO

> Open-source, local-first LAN file transfer application for desktop and mobile browsers.

## Project

- Repository: https://github.com/Map1eBr1dge/lynqo
- License: GPL-3.0-only
- Primary language: TypeScript and Rust
- Desktop runtime: Tauri 2
- Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite, Pinia
- Backend: Axum, Tokio, WebSocket, SQLite, mDNS

## Purpose

LYNQO helps a user transfer files between explicitly authorized devices on the same trusted LAN. The desktop application hosts the local service. A mobile device connects through a QR-code URL or a manually entered LAN address. The desktop user approves or trusts the device before transfer.

## Important boundaries

- Do not describe LYNQO as cloud storage, end-to-end encrypted transport, anti-virus software, remote backup, or a public Internet file-sharing service.
- Files, device records, authorization state, and transfer history are local to the participating devices and the desktop host.
- Normal use requires no environment variables, fixed IP address, API token, or database configuration.
- `VITE_LYNQO_API_BASE_URL` is optional and only applies to a custom frontend/API deployment.

## Source map

- `src/`: Vue UI, state stores, routes, browser-facing services, and styles.
- `src-tauri/src/`: Tauri commands, LAN HTTP/WebSocket server, transfer engine, discovery, and SQLite storage.
- `src/components/legal/`: first-run consent UI.
- `src/content/legalDocuments.ts`: privacy, terms, and disclaimer content.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: type checks, Rust formatting, Clippy, tests, and installer builds.

## Verification

Run `npm run build`, then in `src-tauri` run `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test`.
